zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman於1177年出生在英國爾普雷斯特波利。她和Richard Lyman至少有1個女兒。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman于1177年出生在英国爾普雷斯特波利。她和Richard Lyman至少有1个女儿。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lyman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land (see   Layman  ).
Swedish: habitational name, formed with man ‘man’, for someone from any of several places whose name is beginning with Ly- (e.g. Lyhundra, Lydinge, and Lynäs).

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.

variants.name: Leiman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Menashe, Shoshi, Yehoshua.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Leyman, of uncertain origin.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Leye (English Leah) + man ‘husband of’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman was born in 1177, in Prestbury, Cheshire, England. She had at least 1 daughter with Richard Lyman. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman est née en 1177, à Prestbury, Cheshire, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Richard Lyman. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman wurde 1177 in Prestbury, Cheshire, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Richard Lyman mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman è nata il nel 1177 nel Prestbury, Cheshire, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Richard Lyman. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイア
ja.Prestbury, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアプレストブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lymanは1177年にイギリスチェシュアイアプレストブリーで生まれた。彼女はRichard Lymanとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman(은)는 1177년 영국 체실르 프레스트버이에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Richard Lyman(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman nasceu em 1177, em Prestbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Richard Lyman. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman родилась в 1177 г. в местности престбури, чешир, Англия. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Richard Lyman. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Lyman nació en 1177, en Prestbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Richard Lyman. Murió en su pueblo.
